post.description: See [API Guide: Structured Storage](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/app-dev/structuredstorage/) for more information.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace in which to perform the operation.

post.summary: Create multiple empty application elements at once.
post.description: Call this faster API instead of creating multiple app elements one at a time or in parallel.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace in which to perform the operation.

get.summary: Download the blob element (i.e., a file) stored in an app element in a document's workspace.
get.description: The downloaded file can be used to retrieve stored subelements.

post.summary: Create a new blob subelement from an uploaded file.
post.description: Request body parameters are multipart fields, so you must use `"Content-Type":"multipart/form-data"` in the request header.

delete.summary: Delete a blob subelement from an app element.

get.summary: Get a list of all transactions performed on an element.

post.description: Creates a microbranch (i.e., a branch for a new microversion).

delete.description: Deletes a microbranch (i.e., the branch with the microversion for the transaction).

post.summary: Merge multiple transactions into one microversion.
post.description: If successful, all transactions will be committed to a single microversion. If the call raises an error, nothing will be committed.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace in which to perform the operation.

get.summary: Download a blob subelement from the specified app element.
get.description: Download a blob subelement as a file.

get.summary: Get a list of all blob subelement IDs for the specified workspace, version, or microversion.
Compare app element JSON trees between workspaces/versions/microversions in a document.
get.description: Specify the source workspace/version/microversion in the URL and specify the target workspace/version/microversion in the query parameters.

get.summary: Get a list of all subelement IDs in a specified workspace/version/microversion.

get.description: Resolve all references for all workspace elements. For bulk operations  only. Use `resolveReferences` for a single element.

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The id of the transaction in which this operation should take place. Transaction ids can be generated through the AppElement startTransaction API.

parameters.name: parentChangeId
parameters.description: The id of the last change made to this application element. This can be retrieved from the response for any app element modification endpoint.

parameters.name: includeInternal
parameters.description: Whether to include references that have been deleted or inactivated.

parameters.name: referenceIds
parameters.description: Comma separated string of reference ids find.

parameters.name: elementIds
parameters.description: Comma separated string of element ids to search for references in.

parameters.name: drawingsOnly
parameters.description: Whether to find references for only Onshape drawing app elements.

description.description: The label that will appear in the document's edit history for this operation. If blank, a value will be auto-generated.

formatId.description: The data type of the application. This string allows an application to distinguish their elements from elements of another application.

jsonTree.description: Initialization data for the new element's json tree.

name.description: The name of the element being created. If blank, a name will be auto-generated.

subelements.description: Initialization data for the new element's subelements.

changeId.description: The latest change id for the element, after the edit was committed.

elementId.description: The id of the edited element.

elementIds.description: The ids of the edited elements, if multiple elements were edited.

errorCode.description: `0: OK (healthy) | 1: INFO | 2: WARNING | 3: ERROR (dangling or view generation call failed) | 4: UNKNOWN`

errorDescription.description: A human-readable value for the error that occurred, if one occurred.

parentChangeId.description: The latest change id for the element, before the edit was made.

propertyEditsMerged.description: When committing a transaction, this field indicates if the properties of the application element were changed after the transaction was created.

transactionId.description: The id of the transaction in which the edit was applied.

description.description: The label that will appear in the document's edit history for this operation. If blank, a value will be auto-generated.

formatId.description: The data type of the application. This string allows an application to distinguish their elements from elements of another application.

names.description: The name of the element being created. If blank, a name will be auto-generated.

items.description: The name of the element being created. If blank, a name will be auto-generated.

documentMicroversionId.description: The latest document microversion, after the edit was committed.

elementIds.description: The ids of the created elements.

items.description: The ids of the created elements.

elementMicroversions.description: The microversion ids of the created elements, at creation time.

items.description: The microversion ids of the created elements, at creation time.

documentMicroversionId.description: The latest change id for the document, after the edit was committed.

elementChangeResults.description: The results of editing each element affected by the edit.

jsonPatch.description: A json patch that will be applied to the application element's json data. The JSON patch format is as specified in RFC 6902 from the IETF.

parentChangeId.description: The id of the last change made to this application element. This can be retrieved from the response for any app element modification endpoint.

propertyUpdates.description: Edits to be applied to the element's metadata.

returnError.description: If true, errors in request processing will be returned in a 200 response with a meaningful description. Otherwise, errors will result in a relevant HTTP error response.

returnJsonDifferenceFormat.description: If specified, and jsonTreeEdit is non-empty, the json difference will be returned in this format, otherwise no json difference will be returned.

transactionId.description: The id of the transaction in which this operation should take place. Transaction ids can be generated through the AppElement startTransaction API.

errorCode.description: `0: OK (healthy) | 1: INFO | 2: WARNING | 3: ERROR (dangling or view generation call failed) | 4: UNKNOWN`

errorDescription.description: A human-readable value for the error that occurred, if one occurred.

changeId.description: The latest change id for the element, after the edit was committed.

errorCode.description: `0: OK (healthy) | 1: INFO | 2: WARNING | 3: ERROR (dangling or view generation call failed) | 4: UNKNOWN`

transactionId.description: The id of the transaction in which the edit was applied.

errorDescription.description: A human-readable value for the error that occurred, if one occurred.

targetVersionId.description: Reference to a part or assembly in a version; `NULL` when reference is in a workspace.

elementId.description: The id of an element which provides context for the position value specified.

position.description: An indicator for the relative placement of the new element. If elementId is specified, a negative number indicates insertion prior to the element and a non-negative number indicates insertion following the element. If no elementId is specified, a negative value indicates insertion at the end of the element list and a non-negative number indicates insertion at the start of the element list.

BTElementLocationParams.description: The location at which the new element should be inserted.

baseContent.description: This base64-encoded data is treated as a full representation of the sub-element's data and all deltas previously added will no longer be returned.

delta.description: This base64-encoded data is a delta which the application can apply to the last added baseContent data.

subelementId.description: The id of the subelement to edit. The value is determined by the application.

BTJEdit-3734.description: An edit that will be applied to the application element's json data.

propertyId.description: The id of the property that should be edited. This can be retrieved from MetadataCategory:getCategoryProperties.

value.description: The new value for the property.

scopes.OAuth2ReadPII: Application can read your profile information
scopes.OAuth2Write: Application can write to your documents
scopes.OAuth2Delete: Application can delete your documents and workspaces
scopes.OAuth2Purchase: Application can request purchases on your behalf
scopes.OAuth2Share: Application can share and unshare documents on your behalf
scopes.document.create: Atlas Application can create a document
scopes.document.edit: Atlas Application can edit a document
scopes.document.delete: Atlas Application can delete a document
scopes.document.read: Atlas Application can read a document
scopes.enterprise.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.read: Atlas Application can read from an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.edit: Atlas Application can edit an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.delete: Atlas Application can delete an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.member.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.read: Atlas Application can read the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.edit: Atlas Application can edit the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.delete: Atlas Application can delete a member from an enterprise
scopes.webhook.create: Atlas Application can create a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.edit: Atlas Application can edit a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.read: Atlas Application can read a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.delete: Atlas Application can delete a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.PLMIntegration: PLM automation can invoke limited operations

BasicAuth.description: Use Basic Authentication with API Keys (key as username and secret as password) to authenticate requests.
